items.title: Madrid send UEFA evidence in Vini Jr. racism probe
items.summary: Real Madrid say they have supplied "all available evidence" to UEFA as the governing body investigates allegations of racism made by Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: Fabregas 'not proud' of incident with Milan player
items.summary: Como coach Cesc Fabregas has apologised for making contact with an AC Milan player near the touchline in Wednesday's 1-1 league draw at the San Siro.

items.title: 19 sentenced to jail for AFCON final hooliganism
items.summary: A Moroccan court Thursday sentenced 19 soccer fans to up to a year in prison on charges of hooliganism stemming from a chaotic Africa Cup of Nations final between Morocco and Senegal that saw supporters try to storm the field.

items.title: Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Tottenham’s Tudor age begins with a north London derby, Guardiola ponders Haaland’s role and Wirtz has a chance to flummox Forest Aston Villa, third in the Premier League, chasing Champions League qualification and the Europa League title, will be expected to beat Leeds on home soil. But Unai Emery’s side have struggled of late in games where the pressure is on and the onus is upon them to be the aggressor. After exiting the FA Cup to Newcastle, Marco Bizot’s moment of madness all but ending their hopes of reaching the fifth round, it is back to league duty. They eked out an ugly win over Brighton, just the kind of result they would be happy with this weekend, but recently they also lost at home to 10-man Brentford and to Everton. Before that they drew at lowly Crystal Palace, though Oliver Glasner’s side have been a bogey team for Villa. This week Bizot apologised for his rush of blood. Which Villa will turn up against Leeds? Ben Fisher Aston Villa v Leeds, Saturday 3pm (all times GMT) Brentford v Brighton, Saturday 3pm Chelsea v Burnley, Saturday 3pm West Ham v Bournemouth, Saturday 5.30pm Continue reading...

items.title: F1 chief Domenicali confident Verstappen will not quit
items.summary: Formula 1 president Stefano Domenicali says he is confident Max Verstappen will not leave the sport despite the four-time world champion having recently expressed concern over new regulations for the 2026 season.

items.title: Papers: Alonso rejects job offer over concerns club is 'too chaotic'
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Friday's newspapers...

items.title: Van Ewijk verlengt bij Coventry: 'Dit voelt als een beloning'
items.summary: Milan van Ewijk blijft langer bij Coventry City. Hij heeft zijn contract, dat doorliep tot medio 2027, verlengd. De rechtsback gaat mogelijk komend seizoen de Premier League in met de huidige koploper van het Championship.

items.title: Meet the 19-year-old challenging to make USMNT's World Cup squad
items.summary: Noahkai Banks' strong form in Bundesliga might get him a starting spot on Mauricio Pochettino's USMNT squad.

items.title: No respite for World Cup fans affected by Trump travel restrictions
items.summary: Members of the Haitian and Senegalese communities in the U.S. react to President Donald Trump's visa restrictions ahead of the World Cup.

items.title: 'Opportunity we had to take' - Lewis redeems himself as Hull KR conquer the world
items.summary: Mikey Lewis said winning the World Club Challenge was an opportunity too good to turn down after atoning for his recent indiscretion by helping Hull KR scale the summit of rugby league.

items.title: Isimat-Mirin zet punt achter carrière en krijgt functie in jeugdopleiding PSV
items.summary: Nicolas Isimat-Mirin heeft zijn voetbalcarrière per direct beëindigd. De 34-jarige Fransman zat sinds de zomer zonder club en trainde geruime tijd mee bij Jong PSV om zijn conditie op peil te houden. De hoop op een nieuwe club is nu echter vervlogen, waardoor Isimat-Mirin een ander pad inslaat.

items.title: Clayton wins Premier League in Glasgow after crushing Van Veen and Littler
items.summary: Jonny Clayton enjoyed one of the darting performances of his life to crush Gian van Veen, a misfiring Luke Littler and Gerwyn Price to win the Premier League in Glasgow.

items.title: Forest zet opvallende Europese doelpuntenstatistiek op zijn naam
items.summary: AZ vervolgde donderdagavond het Europese avontuur. De Alkmaarders stonden in de tussenronde van de Conference League tegenover het Armeense FC Noah, maar verloren het heenduel. Volgende week is de return in Alkmaar. Lees alles terug via deze VI Live.

items.title: Van de Donk tóch terug bij Oranje, Janssen en Beerensteyn naar CL-kwartfinales
items.summary: In dit blog volgen we alle ontwikkelingen in het vrouwenvoetbal. We besteden onder meer aandacht aan de Eurojackpot Vrouwen Eredivisie, de Europese toernooien en het Nederlands elftal.

items.title: Meerveld op een missie: 'Mijn ondergrens moet van een 3 naar een 5'
items.summary: Ringo Meerveld heeft zichzelf een duidelijk doel gesteld voor de rest van het seizoen. De middenvelder van SC Heerenveen is druk bezig om stabieler te worden en beseft dat hij te veel mindere wedstrijden gespeeld heeft.

items.title: Celtic face Europa League exit after El Khannouss double sparks Stuttgart rout
items.summary: It is just as well Martin O’Neill wanted no celebration of his 1,000th game in professional management. ­Stuttgart’s visit became men versus Bhoys and a deflating scene for anybody of Celtic persuasion. This messiest of Celtic seasons, featuring umpteen managers and a similar number of low points has now featured supporters booing their own goalkeeper. Fans decided Kasper Schmeichel’s failure to keep out Jamie Leweling’s 57th-minute shot was a blunder too far. Schmeichel’s subsequent touches were jeered, albeit there was nothing he could do about the goal from Tiago Tomás in stoppage time that added gloss to Stuttgart’s position. The second leg feels like a fixture O’Neill could very much do without, coming days before a crucial visit to Ibrox. Continue reading...

items.title: Forest 'enjoy' themselves for perfect start under Pereira
items.summary: Vitor Pereira asks his Nottingham Forest players to "express themselves" prior to their commanding victory at Fenerbahce in the first leg of their Europa League knockout round play-off tie.

items.title: Celtic booed as Stuttgart loss all-but ends their Europa League hopes
items.summary: Celtic's hopes of Europa League progress are all but over after a 4-1 thrashing at home by Stuttgart in their knockout play-off first leg.

items.title: Nottingham Forest dominate Fenerbahce to give Vítor Pereira perfect start
items.summary: For Nottingham Forest, and particularly Vítor Pereira and the 1,200 supporters who signed up to this trip, an evening in Istanbul they will not forget in a hurry. After just three training sessions, Pereira’s first match in charge could scarcely have been more impressive than the statement victory Forest recorded at Fenerbahce to put themselves in command to reach the Europa League last 16. By the end, the yellow and navy stronghold had emptied and the Forest fans present savoured every moment. “Can we play you every week?” they sang, and then: “Where’s your famous atmosphere?” Continue reading...

items.title: Women's FA Cup revamp on hold after 'ridiculous idea' criticism
items.summary: Plans to change the format of the Women's FA Cup, which would have seen four sides seeded and pathways to Wembley mapped out from the last 32 onwards, will not be brought in next season.
